hero.title: Asya Oleynichenko
hero.text: Five years in product design. Now on upsell and offer personalization at VK: monetization for Mail Space, Cloud, and Mail. Previously ~1.5 years on Mail media (Mail Science, Hi-Tech, Cinema, Lady, Pets). Work spans research, hypotheses, A/B tests, design, and impact on LTV, conversion, retention, and ARPPU. DAFES AWARDS finalist & winner · HSE red diploma, GPA 8.82 (Design & Programming). Open to Senior Product Designer roles.

intro.name: Asya Oleynichenko
intro.lead1: Product designer at VK: monetization for Mail Space, Cloud, and Mail; before that — Mail media (Science, Hi-Tech, special projects). Five years across research, hypotheses, A/B tests, launches, and metrics.
intro.lead2: DAFES AWARDS finalist & winner; HSE red diploma (GPA 8.82). Open to Senior Product Designer roles and strong product teams.

skills.title: Skills
items: Product: research through hypothesis testing and metric impact
Hypotheses & experiments: A/B, analytics, data-informed decisions
Research: competitors, depth interviews, scenarios, growth points
Art direction: led Mail Media special projects
AI in production: Cursor, Midjourney in the pipeline, editorial training
Engineering literacy: basic coding (HSE), layout, partnering with dev

team.title: Team
team.body: I work in cross-functional teams with PMs, analytics, engineering, and researchers — from hypothesis to experiment readout

habitat.title: Beyond work
habitat.body: City life, music, internet visual noise, and an endless stream of references — where I recharge for interfaces and art direction

memes.title: Favorite memes by imaginary people
memes.body: A stash of absurd images and quotes without context — a reminder that products live in culture, not only in dashboards

jobs.title: Product designer
jobs.text: Upsell & offer personalization team: Mail Space, Cloud, Mail. A/B experiments with tens of millions of ₽ incremental LTV (NDA).

jobs.title: Product designer
jobs.text: Mail Science launch, Media UI, redesigns for Hi-Tech, Cinema, Lady, Pets. Led special projects: 23 launches in 3 months.

jobs.title: Marketing intern
jobs.text: Worked on the mobile app design system.

eduItems.line: Faculty of communications, media, and design

eduItems.detail: Product approach to leadership: metrics, goals, scoping, process

mail-nauki.title: Mail media redesign & Mail Science launch
mail-nauki.desc: New Mail Science vertical + Media UI foundation for platform-wide redesign
introParas: Mail Media is five thematic products combining editorial, recommendations, and ads.
The goal was to launch Mail Science and use it as the base architecture for a systematic platform redesign.

mail-spetsproekty.title: Growing the special-projects stream
mail-spetsproekty.desc: Branded specials: tighter process, AI in the workflow, less outsourcing
introParas: I led the special-projects stream and built a scalable system for producing branded content—faster launches, higher throughput, and less reliance on contractors.

mail-monetization.title: Mail monetization
mail-monetization.meta: Growth · Mail Space, Mail, Cloud · 2025 — present
mail-monetization.desc: Experiments: plans, contextual upsell, sharing limits, ML freemium
introParas: Scope: monetization scenarios, A/B tests, analytics, engineering and research. Surfaces: Mail Space, Mail, and Cloud—plans, subscription entry points, ML freemium, multi-device limits.

drop.title: DROP — networking for city life
drop.desc: Location-based networking: iOS concept and a Telegram MVP
introParas: DROP is a digital service for city networking: people leave anonymous notes pinned to real places. A note can be text, a voice clip, or a small 3D object tied to a location. Anyone nearby can discover it, react, or start a conversation—adding a digital layer on top of the city where physical spots become anchors for unexpected social interactions.

loochok.title: LÒÒCHOK — customization & DIY community
loochok.desc: 0→1: DIY platform, community, web + iOS, pitch
introParas: The core idea is a dedicated space where people can find customization ideas, follow step-by-step guides, and share what they make.
The project grew as a media ecosystem—content, community, and tools for participants to connect.

neural.title: Neural networks — Mail Hi-Tech editorial special
neural.desc: Interactive landing that proved the format and kicked off branded specials
introParas: Interest in neural networks was rising, but standard text-led editorial formats didn’t drive enough engagement—the topic felt too complex and lacked visual storytelling. That meant weak time-on-page and shallow reading depth.

inkz.title: INKZ — find your tattoo artist
inkz.desc: Two-month 0→1: research, MVP, monetization for artists
introParas: INKZ is a tattoo-artist discovery platform concept built from zero in two months. The hypothesis: reviews, ratings, and style-based search make it easier to pick an artist and build trust. The goal was an MVP that makes finding an artist transparent and straightforward.

biohacking.title: Immersive ad mechanics: “Biohacking” for Invitro
biohacking.desc: Readymag + Spline: a native brand inside an editorial longread
introParas: I designed an interactive special project for Invitro—editorial content, 3D motion, and native brand integration in one experience.

retrash.title: RE*TRASH — media about mindful consumption
retrash.desc: Product + brand: sustainability without the lecture
introParas: A media concept to make mindful consumption easier through structured content, how-tos, and a visual system—spanning product, editorial, and brand.

racktables.title: RackTables: redesigning infrastructure management
racktables.desc: Fast Track: from a passive database to solution support in two days
introParas: Shipped in two days as part of Yandex Fast Track. RackTables had behaved like a passive database: information was structured linearly with no clear priority. During incidents and outages that meant critical delay—engineers spent time manually searching and interpreting object state.
